NCT01949948.name: NOR-TEST
NCT01949948.group: AIS <=4.5 h, tenecteplase 0.4 mg/kg vs alteplase 0.9 mg/kg (90-day mRS 0-1 RR, non-inferiority)
NCT01949948.snippet: Source: Logallo N et al. Lancet Neurol 2017;16:781-788 (NOR-TEST).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,100 adults with acute ischaemic stroke within 4.5 hours of symptom onset (or within 4.5 hours of stroke-on-awakening when clear last-known-well available) randomized 1:1 PROBE (open-label treatment, blinded outcome) to tenecteplase 0.4 mg/kg IV bolus (n=549) or alteplase 0.9 mg/kg IV (10% bolus + 90-min infusion, n=542). Minor-stroke-dominant population (median NIHSS 4).

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-1
evidence.text: Excellent functional outcome (modified Rankin Scale 0-1 at 90 days): tenecteplase 358/549 (64%) vs alteplase 330/542 (63%); risk ratio 1.08 (95% CI 0.96-1.20). Non-inferiority of tenecteplase demonstrated within pre-specified margin.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage (SITS-MOST definition): tenecteplase 3% vs alteplase 2% (NS). 90-day mortality: 5% vs 5% (NS). No excess serious adverse events. Subgroup effect largely consistent across NIHSS strata.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - PROBE design (open-label treatment allocation, but outcome blinded). D3 LOW - 99% mRS ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded 90-day mRS adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked. Higher 0.4 mg/kg dose was unique to NOR-TEST; subsequent NOR-TEST 2A at same dose terminated for harm in severe-stroke population.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Age 18 years or older~* Ischaemic stroke with measurable deficit on NIH Stroke Scale~* All stroke sub-types, severities and vascular distributions,a visible arterial occlusion is not required for inclusion~* Treatment within 4 ½ hours of stroke onset~* Patients awakening with symptoms are defined by the time last observed normal and awake~* Informed written consent signed by the patient, verbal consent from the patients as witnessed by a non-participating health care perso...

NCT03889249.name: AcT
NCT03889249.group: AIS <=4.5 h, tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg vs alteplase 0.9 mg/kg (90-day mRS 0-1 RR, non-inferiority)
NCT03889249.snippet: Source: Menon BK et al. Lancet 2022;400:161-169 (AcT).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,577 adults with acute ischaemic stroke eligible for IV thrombolysis within 4.5 hours of onset randomized 1:1 PROBE in Canadian centres to tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg IV bolus (n=802) or alteplase 0.9 mg/kg (n=765). Broader population than NOR-TEST (median NIHSS 9); 33% received concurrent thrombectomy.

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-1
evidence.text: Excellent functional outcome (mRS 0-1 at 90 days): tenecteplase 296/802 (36.9%) vs alteplase 266/765 (34.8%); risk ratio 1.06 (95% CI 0.95-1.19; unadjusted). Adjusted risk difference +2.1% (-2.6 to +6.9). Non-inferiority of tenecteplase met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH, Mortality, and EVT Subgroup
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: tenecteplase 3.4% vs alteplase 3.2% (NS). 90-day mortality: 15.3% vs 15.4% (NS). Benefit consistent across EVT and non-EVT subgroups.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by centre. D2 LOW - PROBE with blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 100% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded 90-day mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria: Inclusion criteria is pragmatic and informed by Canadian Best Practices.~* All patients with acute ischemic stroke eligible to receive intravenous alteplase as per standard care will be eligible for enrolment in the proposed trial.~* Patients eligible for endovascular thrombectomy in addition to intravenous thrombolysis are eligible for enrolment.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Contra-indications to intravenous thrombolysis as used by treating physicians as current standard of care a...

NCT04797013.name: TRACE-2
NCT04797013.group: AIS <=4.5 h in China, tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg vs alteplase 0.9 mg/kg (90-day mRS 0-1 RR, non-inferiority)
NCT04797013.snippet: Source: Wang Y et al. Lancet 2023;401:645-654 (TRACE-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,430 adults with acute ischaemic stroke within 4.5 hours of onset and not planned for thrombectomy enrolled in 53 Chinese centres randomized 1:1 PROBE to tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg IV bolus (n=708) or alteplase 0.9 mg/kg (n=700). Mild-to-moderate stroke population (median NIHSS 7).

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-1
evidence.text: Excellent functional outcome (mRS 0-1 at 90 days): tenecteplase 439/708 (62.0%) vs alteplase 415/700 (59.3%); risk ratio 1.05 (95% CI 0.96-1.13). Non-inferiority met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: tenecteplase 2.1% vs alteplase 1.3% (NS). 90-day mortality: 7.4% vs 5.3% (NS). Safety consistent with prior trials at 0.25 mg/kg dose.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 LOW - PROBE with blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 99% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Age ≥18 years old, no gender limitation;~* The time from onset to treatment was < 4.5h;The time at which symptoms begin is defined as the time at which they finally appear normal;~* The clinical diagnosis was ischemic stroke (the diagnosis followed the Chinese Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Treatment of Acute Ischemic Stroke 2018);~* MRS before onset was 0-1 points~* Baseline NIHSS 5-25(both included);~* Informed consent from the patient or surrogate.~Exclusion Criteria...
